project.name: fastapi-backend
project.description: Production-ready FastAPI backend with layered architecture

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the alembic migrations directory. Contains no logic.

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Alembic migration environment configuration. Auto-discovers all module models, wires SQLModel metadata as the migration target, and provides both offline and online migration execution paths.

nodes.name: 20260321_0001_initial_schema.py
nodes.summary: Initial Alembic migration (revision 0001) that creates the `user` and `item` tables with soft-delete support, unique/partial indexes, and a foreign-key relationship from item to user.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the top-level app package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.api sub-package.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency providers for the API layer. Exports `SessionDep`, `TokenDep`, `CurrentUser`, and `CurrentSuperuser` type aliases built with `Annotated[..., Depends(...)]`, plus the `get_current_user` and `get_current_superuser` dependency functions.

nodes.name: docs.py
nodes.summary: Shared OpenAPI response-body constants (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, BAD_REQUEST, UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY, CONFLICT) used as `responses=` metadata in FastAPI route definitions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.api.v1 sub-package.

nodes.name: run_migrations_offline
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations in offline mode using a URL string without an active database connection.

nodes.name: run_migrations_online
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations in online mode by creating an engine connection and applying changes within a transaction.

nodes.name: upgrade
nodes.summary: Creates the `user` and `item` tables and their indexes as part of the initial schema migration.

nodes.name: downgrade
nodes.summary: Drops the `item` and `user` tables to roll back the initial schema migration.

nodes.name: get_current_user
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that resolves and returns the currently authenticated active user by delegating to the auth service.

nodes.name: get_current_superuser
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that ensures the current user has superuser privileges, raising PermissionDeniedException otherwise.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the v1 endpoints sub-package.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router defining authentication HTTP endpoints: login (POST /login, rate-limited 5/min), token refresh (POST /refresh, 20/min), logout (POST /logout), and self-service signup (POST /signup, 3/min). Delegates all business logic to app.modules.auth.service and app.modules.users.service.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: POST /login handler. Authenticates credentials via authenticate_user, then creates and returns a JWT access+refresh token pair. Raises AuthException on bad credentials. Rate-limited to 5 requests/minute.

nodes.name: health.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router providing a single GET / health-check endpoint that verifies database connectivity and returns HealthStatus. Returns HTTP 503 with status 'error' if the database is unreachable.

nodes.name: items.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router for the items resource, exposing CRUD endpoints: create (POST /), list own (GET /my), get by id (GET /{item_id}), update (PUT /{item_id}), delete (DELETE /{item_id}), and superuser list-all (GET /). Enforces ownership and superuser permissions via service layer.

nodes.name: users.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router for user management: GET /me returns current user profile, PUT /me updates editable fields, GET / lists all users with pagination and filters (superuser only).

nodes.name: read_users
nodes.summary: GET / handler that returns a paginated list of all users. Requires superuser authority (CurrentSuperuser). Delegates to list_users_with_count and wraps result in a Page response.

nodes.name: router.py
nodes.summary: V1 API router aggregator. Creates api_router and includes the auth, users, items, and health sub-routers under their respective path prefixes and OpenAPI tags.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the core sub-package.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-Settings based application configuration. Defines the Settings class with all environment variables for database, JWT, Redis, CORS, and superuser bootstrap. Provides a cached get_settings() factory and a module-level settings singleton.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings subclass that reads all application configuration from environment variables and .env file. Computes DATABASE_URL from individual Postgres fields, validates CORS origins (no wildcards, must be http/https), parses comma-separated list fields, and exposes an IS_PRODUCTION computed boolean.

nodes.name: context.py
nodes.summary: Defines a ContextVar `request_id_ctx` that stores the current request ID string across async boundaries, enabling request-scoped tracing throughout the middleware and logging layers.

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: Manages the SQLAlchemy/SQLModel engine and session lifecycle. Provides `get_engine` (cached singleton), `reset_engine` (for test teardown), `get_session` (session generator), and `check_database_health` (single-query liveness probe). Uses NullPool in test environments and QueuePool in production.

nodes.name: limiter.py
nodes.summary: Instantiates the SlowAPI `Limiter` singleton configured with `get_remote_address` as key function and Redis as storage backend. No default limits are set; individual endpoints opt in.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Sets up structured JSON logging for the `app` logger using `python-json-logger`. Defines `RequestIDFilter` to inject the current request ID from `request_id_ctx` into every log record, and `setup_logging` to configure handler, formatter, and filter.

nodes.name: redis.py
nodes.summary: Manages a lazily-initialised Redis `ConnectionPool` singleton. Exposes `get_redis()` to obtain a connected `redis.Redis` client and `close_redis()` to gracefully shut down the pool on application teardown.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: JWT dual-token creation (`create_access_token`, `create_refresh_token`) and decoding (`decode_token`), bcrypt password hashing/verification (`verify_password`, `get_password_hash`), password strength validation (`validate_password_strength`), and a timing-safe dummy hash constant. Defines custom `InvalidTokenError`.

nodes.name: transaction.py
nodes.summary: Provides two SQLModel session transaction helpers: `safe_commit` (commit with automatic rollback on `SQLAlchemyError`) and `commit_and_refresh` (commit then refresh one or more model instances, returning the first).

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application factory. Registers the full middleware chain (ProxyHeaders, RequestID, SecurityHeaders, Logging, CORS), attaches the SlowAPI rate-limit handler, registers exception handlers, includes the v1 API router, and exposes a root health-check endpoint. Lifespan closes Redis on shutdown.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initialiser for the middleware package.

nodes.name: _build_engine_kwargs
nodes.summary: Builds the SQLAlchemy engine keyword arguments dict, selecting NullPool for test environments and QueuePool with configurable pool size for all other environments.

nodes.name: get_engine
nodes.summary: Returns the cached SQLAlchemy Engine singleton, creating it from settings on first call.

nodes.name: reset_engine
nodes.summary: Disposes the cached engine and clears the reference. Used in test teardown to force re-creation of the engine when settings change.

nodes.name: get_session
nodes.summary: Generator that yields a SQLModel Session bound to the singleton engine, for use as a FastAPI dependency.

nodes.name: check_database_health
nodes.summary: Executes a single `SELECT 1` to verify database connectivity. Designed to be called once by an external probe (K8s liveness/readiness); does not retry internally.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures the `app` logger with a JSON formatter, stdout handler, and the `RequestIDFilter`. Idempotent — returns immediately if handlers are already registered.

nodes.name: _get_pool
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and returns the Redis `ConnectionPool` singleton from `settings.REDIS_URL`.

nodes.name: get_redis
nodes.summary: Returns a `redis.Redis` client connected to the shared connection pool.

nodes.name: close_redis
nodes.summary: Disconnects all connections in the pool and clears the singleton reference. Called during application shutdown lifespan.

nodes.name: create_access_token
nodes.summary: Creates a signed JWT access token for the given subject with a configurable expiry (default from settings).

nodes.name: create_refresh_token
nodes.summary: Creates a signed JWT refresh token and returns `(token, jti)`. The caller must store the jti in Redis for revocation tracking.

nodes.name: verify_password
nodes.summary: Verifies a plain-text password against a bcrypt hash, using DUMMY_HASH as a timing-safe fallback when the stored hash is absent.

nodes.name: get_password_hash
nodes.summary: Hashes a plain-text password with bcrypt and returns the encoded string.

nodes.name: validate_password_strength
nodes.summary: Validates that a password contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit. Raises ValueError on failure.

nodes.name: decode_token
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates a JWT token, checking the expected `type` claim. Raises `InvalidTokenError` on any JWT decode or validation failure.

nodes.name: safe_commit
nodes.summary: Commits the session; rolls back and re-raises on `SQLAlchemyError`. For mutations that do not require instance refresh (e.g. deletes).

nodes.name: commit_and_refresh
nodes.summary: Commits the session and refreshes one or more SQLModel instances from the DB. Returns the first instance for caller convenience.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: Async context manager used as FastAPI lifespan hook. Yields on startup and calls `close_redis()` on shutdown.

nodes.name: root
nodes.summary: GET `/` endpoint returning a static welcome message. Serves as a basic liveness indicator at the root path.

nodes.name: RequestIDFilter
nodes.summary: A `logging.Filter` subclass that injects the current `request_id` from `request_id_ctx` ContextVar into every `LogRecord`, enabling per-request tracing in structured log output.

nodes.name: InvalidTokenError
nodes.summary: Custom exception raised by `decode_token` when a JWT is malformed, expired, or has an unexpected type claim.

nodes.name: _types.py
nodes.summary: Defines shared ASGI type aliases (_Scope, _Message, _Receive, _Send, _ASGIApp) used by all middleware components.

nodes.name: error_handler.py
nodes.summary: Registers a global FastAPI exception handler that converts AppException subclasses into structured JSON responses with appropriate HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI middleware that logs structured JSON request/response records including method, path, status code, duration, and request ID.

nodes.name: request_id.py
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI middleware that generates a UUID per request, stores it in a context variable, and injects it into the X-Request-Id response header.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI middleware that appends security response headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, referrer policy, permissions policy) to every HTTP response.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package docstring explains that model aggregation imports are intentionally absent here to avoid circular imports; Alembic autogenerate imports are handled explicitly in alembic/env.py.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines TableBase, the soft-delete SQLModel base class providing id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at fields and a pk property. Also provides utc_now helper.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the modules namespace.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the auth module namespace.

nodes.name: register_exception_handlers
nodes.summary: Registers the AppException handler on a FastAPI app instance, mapping application-level exceptions to JSON responses with status codes and detail fields.

nodes.name: LoggingMiddleware
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI callable middleware class that wraps each HTTP request in a timing context and emits a structured JSON log entry on completion, using the request_id context variable.

nodes.name: RequestIDMiddleware
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI callable middleware that generates a UUID4 per HTTP request, stores it in request_id_ctx, and injects it as X-Request-Id in the response headers.

nodes.name: SecurityHeadersMiddleware
nodes.summary: Pure-ASGI callable middleware that appends a fixed set of security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) to every HTTP response.

nodes.name: TableBase
nodes.summary: Abstract SQLModel base class providing id (primary key), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at (soft delete) fields and a pk property that raises if the entity has not been flushed.

nodes.name: utc_now
nodes.summary: Returns the current UTC datetime; used as a default_factory for created_at and updated_at fields.

nodes.name: auth/schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for JWT authentication: Token (access+refresh tokens response), TokenPayload (JWT claims), and RefreshTokenRequest (refresh token input).

nodes.name: Token
nodes.summary: Response schema returning access_token, refresh_token, and token_type fields for a successful login or token refresh.

nodes.name: TokenPayload
nodes.summary: Internal schema representing decoded JWT claims: sub (user ID) and type (access or refresh).

nodes.name: RefreshTokenRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema accepting a refresh_token string to exchange for new tokens.

nodes.name: auth/service.py
nodes.summary: Authentication business logic: user login, JWT token creation, token refresh with Redis JTI tracking, logout (token revocation), and current-user resolution.

nodes.name: _extract_user_id
nodes.summary: Extracts and validates the 'sub' claim from a decoded JWT payload, returning the integer user ID.

nodes.name: _store_refresh_jti
nodes.summary: Stores a refresh token JTI in Redis with a TTL matching the configured refresh token expiry.

nodes.name: _verify_refresh_jti
nodes.summary: Checks whether a refresh token JTI still exists in Redis (i.e., has not been revoked).

nodes.name: _revoke_refresh_jti
nodes.summary: Deletes a refresh token JTI from Redis, effectively revoking it.

nodes.name: authenticate_user
nodes.summary: Looks up a user by username and verifies the password using constant-time comparison; returns the User or None.

nodes.name: create_user_token
nodes.summary: Creates a new access+refresh token pair for a user and persists the refresh JTI in Redis.

nodes.name: refresh_user_token
nodes.summary: Validates a refresh token, verifies its JTI in Redis, revokes the old token, and issues a new token pair.

nodes.name: logout_user
nodes.summary: Revokes the provided refresh token by deleting its JTI from Redis after verifying it belongs to the requesting user.

nodes.name: get_current_active_user
nodes.summary: Decodes an access token, retrieves and validates the corresponding user, raising AuthException if inactive or not found.

nodes.name: items/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init file for the items module.

nodes.name: items/deps.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency providers for the items module: parses and validates query parameters into an ItemListParams object via Depends().

nodes.name: get_item_list_params
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that parses skip, limit, sort_by, sort_order, and search query parameters into an ItemListParams instance.

nodes.name: items/models.py
nodes.summary: SQLModel ORM definition for the Item table, inheriting TableBase for soft-delete support, with title, description, and owner_id fields.

nodes.name: Item
nodes.summary: ORM model for the items table. Has title (indexed, max 255), description (nullable), and owner_id (FK to user.id). Includes a partial index on non-deleted rows.

nodes.name: items/repository.py
nodes.summary: ItemRepository extending RepositoryBase[Item] with filtering by owner_id and title search, plus paginated multi-fetch with total count.

nodes.name: ItemRepository
nodes.summary: Data-access class for Item records. Provides _build_filtered_statement for owner/search filtering and get_multi_with_count for paginated listing.

nodes.name: items/schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for item CRUD: ItemBase, ItemCreate, ItemUpdate, ItemResponse, and ItemListParams with sort/search fields.

nodes.name: ItemBase
nodes.summary: Base Pydantic model for items with title and optional description fields.

nodes.name: ItemCreate
nodes.summary: Schema for item creation requests, inheriting all fields from ItemBase.

nodes.name: ItemUpdate
nodes.summary: Schema for item update requests with all optional fields for partial updates.

nodes.name: ItemResponse
nodes.summary: Schema for item API responses including id and owner_id, configured for ORM mode.

nodes.name: ItemListParams
nodes.summary: Pagination and filtering parameters for listing items: inherits PaginationParams, adds sort_by, sort_order, and optional search.

nodes.name: items/service.py
nodes.summary: Business logic for item CRUD operations with ownership enforcement via _check_owner and soft-delete support.

nodes.name: _get_item_or_404
nodes.summary: Fetches an item by ID and raises NotFoundException if not found.

nodes.name: _check_owner
nodes.summary: Raises PermissionDeniedException if the item does not belong to the requesting user and the user is not a superuser.

nodes.name: create_item
nodes.summary: Creates a new Item record owned by owner_id and commits/refreshes the session.

nodes.name: get_item
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single item by ID, enforcing ownership check before returning.

nodes.name: update_item
nodes.summary: Updates an item by ID after verifying ownership, then commits and refreshes.

nodes.name: delete_item
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes an item by ID after verifying ownership.

nodes.name: list_items_with_count
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated list of items along with total count, optionally filtered by owner_id.

nodes.name: users/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init file for the users module.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency providers for user list query parameters. Defines get_user_list_params() and the UserListDep annotated type alias used in user list endpoints.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: SQLModel ORM definition for the User model. Inherits TableBase for soft-delete and audit fields. Defines username, hashed_password, full_name, is_active, is_superuser columns with a unique index on username.

nodes.name: repository.py
nodes.summary: UserRepository extending RepositoryBase[User] with user-specific CRUD: filtered listing with search/is_active/is_superuser, get_by_username, get_active_users, and get_multi_with_count for paginated responses.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for user CRUD operations: UserBase, UserCreate, UserUpdate, UserResponse, UserListParams, and UserSortField literal type.

nodes.name: service.py
nodes.summary: Business logic layer for user management. Functions: create_user, create_superuser, update_user, list_users_with_count. Validates password strength, checks for duplicate usernames, hashes passwords, and delegates persistence to user_repository.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the repositories package.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Generic RepositoryBase[ModelType] providing standard CRUD operations with soft-delete, pagination, and sorting for all SQLModel entities. Foundation for all module-specific repositories.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the schemas package.

nodes.name: common.py
nodes.summary: Shared Pydantic response models used across the application: Page[T] for paginated results, PaginationParams for query parameters, Message, ErrorResponse, HealthStatus, and SortOrder literal type.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file for the app.utils package.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the application exception hierarchy: AppException base class plus specialized subclasses for database, auth, not-found, validation, conflict, and permission-denied errors. Each subclass carries an appropriate HTTP status code.

nodes.name: get_user_list_params
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency function that collects user list query parameters (skip, limit, sort_by, sort_order, search, is_active, is_superuser) from the request and returns a UserListParams instance.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: SQLModel ORM entity for users. Inherits TableBase (id, timestamps, soft-delete). Fields: username (unique), hashed_password, full_name, is_active, is_superuser. Mapped to the 'user' table.

nodes.name: UserRepository
nodes.summary: Data access layer for User entities. Extends RepositoryBase with user-specific filtering (search by username/full_name, is_active, is_superuser), get_by_username lookup, get_active_users batch query, and get_multi_with_count for paginated listing.

nodes.name: UserBase
nodes.summary: Base Pydantic model for user data. Fields: username (1-255 chars), full_name (optional, max 255 chars).

nodes.name: UserCreate
nodes.summary: Schema for user creation requests. Extends UserBase with required password field (8-40 chars).

nodes.name: UserUpdate
nodes.summary: Schema for user update requests. All fields optional: full_name and password. Only explicitly provided fields are updated.

nodes.name: UserResponse
nodes.summary: Schema for user API responses. Extends UserBase with id, is_active, is_superuser. Uses from_attributes=True for ORM compatibility.

nodes.name: UserListParams
nodes.summary: Query parameter schema for listing users. Extends PaginationParams with sort_by (UserSortField), search string, is_active filter, and is_superuser filter.

nodes.name: create_user
nodes.summary: Creates a new standard user. Validates password strength, checks for duplicate username, hashes password, and persists via user_repository.

nodes.name: create_superuser
nodes.summary: Creates a new superuser account. Validates and builds user data then sets is_superuser=True.

nodes.name: update_user
nodes.summary: Updates an existing user's mutable fields (full_name, password). Hashes new password if provided, then commits via repository.

nodes.name: list_users_with_count
nodes.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of users with total count by delegating to UserRepository.get_multi_with_count using UserListParams.

nodes.name: _validate_and_build_user_data
nodes.summary: Internal helper that validates password strength, checks username uniqueness, and returns a dict of user fields ready for model creation.

nodes.name: RepositoryBase
nodes.summary: Generic CRUD repository base class parameterized by ModelType (bound to TableBase). Provides: get, get_multi, count, create, update, soft_delete, _apply_sort, _count_statement, and _escape_like static helper. Handles soft-delete filtering transparently.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Simple Pydantic response model with a single message string field for generic API responses.

nodes.name: ErrorResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for API error responses. Contains a human-readable detail string.

nodes.name: HealthStatus
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for health check endpoints. Contains status and database status strings.

nodes.name: Page
nodes.summary: Generic paginated response model Page[T]. Contains items list, total count, skip, limit, and computed fields: current_page, total_pages, has_next, has_previous.

nodes.name: PaginationParams
nodes.summary: Base Pydantic model for pagination query parameters: skip (default 0), limit (default 100, max 100), sort_by (default 'created_at'), sort_order (default 'desc').

nodes.name: AppException
nodes.summary: Base application exception. Carries a detail message and HTTP status_code=400. All other app exceptions inherit from this.

nodes.name: DatabaseException
nodes.summary: Database-related exception with status_code=503. Raised when database operations fail.

nodes.name: AuthException
nodes.summary: Authentication exception with status_code=401. Raised on invalid credentials or expired tokens.

nodes.name: NotFoundException
nodes.summary: Resource not found exception with status_code=404. Raised when a requested resource does not exist.

nodes.name: ValidationException
nodes.summary: Validation exception with status_code=400. Raised when input data fails business-level validation.

nodes.name: ConflictException
nodes.summary: Conflict exception with status_code=409. Raised on resource conflicts such as duplicate username.

nodes.name: PermissionDeniedException
nodes.summary: Permission denied exception with status_code=403. Raised when an authenticated user lacks the required permission.

nodes.name: bootstrap_db.py
nodes.summary: Initialization script that creates the first superuser on first run. Idempotent — skips creation if the superuser already exists. Depends on user_repository, UserCreate schema, create_superuser service, and app settings.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Opens a database session and creates the first superuser from settings if not already present. Prints status messages and returns early if user exists.

nodes.name: entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Production Docker entrypoint shell script. Runs Alembic migrations, bootstraps initial data, then starts gunicorn with UvicornWorker. Worker count driven by WEB_CONCURRENCY env var (default 4).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file for the tests package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file for the tests.api package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file for the tests.api.v1 package.

nodes.name: test_auth.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the auth endpoints (signup, login, token refresh, logout). Covers happy paths plus security edge cases: duplicate username conflict, invalid token rejection, refresh token rotation, cross-user logout prevention, unauthenticated logout rejection.

nodes.name: test_signup
nodes.summary: Verifies POST /api/v1/auth/signup returns 201 and echoes the username.

nodes.name: test_login
nodes.summary: Verifies POST /api/v1/auth/login returns 200 with access_token and refresh_token.

nodes.name: test_signup_duplicate_username_returns_conflict
nodes.summary: Verifies that signing up with an existing username returns HTTP 409 Conflict.

nodes.name: test_refresh_token
nodes.summary: Verifies POST /api/v1/auth/refresh returns new access and refresh tokens.

nodes.name: test_invalid_refresh_token_returns_unauthorized
nodes.summary: Verifies that using an invalid refresh token returns HTTP 401.

nodes.name: test_refresh_token_cannot_access_protected_route
nodes.summary: Verifies that a refresh token cannot be used as a bearer token for protected routes.

nodes.name: test_refresh_token_rotation_invalidates_old_token
nodes.summary: Verifies that after token rotation the old refresh token is revoked and cannot be reused (returns 401 on replay).

nodes.name: test_logout_revokes_refresh_token
nodes.summary: Verifies that POST /api/v1/auth/logout (204) revokes the refresh token so it cannot be used again.

nodes.name: test_logout_rejects_other_users_token
nodes.summary: Verifies that a user cannot logout using another user's refresh token (returns 401).

nodes.name: test_logout_without_auth_returns_unauthorized
nodes.summary: Verifies that a logout request with no Authorization header returns 401.

nodes.name: test_health.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the health check endpoint, verifying 200 OK on healthy state and 503 on unhealthy state.

nodes.name: test_health_check_ok
nodes.summary: Asserts the health endpoint returns HTTP 200 when all services are healthy.

nodes.name: test_health_check_unhealthy_returns_503
nodes.summary: Monkeypatches the health checker to simulate failure and asserts a 503 response.

nodes.name: test_items.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration tests for items CRUD endpoints covering create, read, update, delete, list, pagination, search, auth enforcement, and permission boundaries.

nodes.name: auth_headers
nodes.summary: pytest fixture that registers a regular user and returns their JWT auth headers.

nodes.name: other_auth_headers
nodes.summary: pytest fixture that registers a second regular user and returns their JWT auth headers for cross-user permission tests.

nodes.name: test_create_item
nodes.summary: Tests successful item creation with title and description for an authenticated user.

nodes.name: test_create_item_without_description
nodes.summary: Tests item creation succeeds when the optional description field is omitted.

nodes.name: test_create_item_requires_auth
nodes.summary: Asserts unauthenticated item creation returns 401.

nodes.name: test_create_item_empty_title_returns_422
nodes.summary: Verifies that an empty title triggers a 422 validation error.

nodes.name: test_read_own_item
nodes.summary: Tests that a user can retrieve their own item by ID.

nodes.name: test_read_other_user_item_returns_403
nodes.summary: Asserts that reading another user's item returns 403 Forbidden.

nodes.name: test_superuser_can_read_any_item
nodes.summary: Verifies a superuser can read items owned by other users.

nodes.name: test_read_nonexistent_item_returns_404
nodes.summary: Asserts that requesting a non-existent item ID returns 404.

nodes.name: test_update_own_item
nodes.summary: Tests full update of an item owned by the authenticated user.

nodes.name: test_update_other_user_item_returns_403
nodes.summary: Asserts that updating another user's item returns 403 Forbidden.

nodes.name: test_partial_update_item
nodes.summary: Tests PATCH partial update of an item's individual fields.

nodes.name: test_delete_own_item
nodes.summary: Tests that a user can soft-delete their own item and it becomes inaccessible.

nodes.name: test_delete_other_user_item_returns_403
nodes.summary: Asserts that deleting another user's item returns 403 Forbidden.

nodes.name: test_list_my_items
nodes.summary: Tests that listing items returns only items belonging to the authenticated user.

nodes.name: test_list_my_items_excludes_others
nodes.summary: Verifies items from other users are excluded from the current user's list.

nodes.name: test_list_my_items_with_search
nodes.summary: Tests title-based search filtering in the items list endpoint.

nodes.summary: Tests pagination (offset/limit) behaviour in the items list endpoint.

nodes.summary: Asserts that the admin list-all-items endpoint returns 403 for regular users.

nodes.name: test_list_all_items_as_superuser
nodes.summary: Verifies that superusers can retrieve all items across all users.

nodes.name: test_users.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for users endpoints covering profile updates, password changes, pagination, search, and boolean filter queries.

nodes.name: user_auth_headers
nodes.summary: pytest fixture that registers a regular user and returns their JWT auth headers.

nodes.name: test_update_user_me_full_name
nodes.summary: Tests that a user can update their own full name via the /me endpoint.

nodes.name: test_update_user_me_password
nodes.summary: Tests successful password change and verifies the new password is accepted on login.

nodes.name: test_update_user_me_weak_password_returns_400
nodes.summary: Asserts that setting a weak password returns HTTP 400.

nodes.name: test_read_users_requires_superuser
nodes.summary: Asserts that the user list endpoint is restricted to superusers.

nodes.name: test_read_users_returns_paginated_response_for_superuser
nodes.summary: Tests paginated user listing for superusers, verifying response shape and item count.

nodes.name: test_read_users_rejects_limit_above_max
nodes.summary: Verifies that requesting a page limit above the allowed maximum returns 422.

nodes.name: test_read_users_supports_search
nodes.summary: Tests username/email search filtering in the superuser user list endpoint.

nodes.name: test_read_users_supports_boolean_filters
nodes.summary: Tests is_active and is_superuser boolean query filters on the user list endpoint.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Root pytest conftest providing session-scoped DB engine (testcontainers PostgreSQL), FakeRedis stub, rate-limit disabling, session fixture, TestClient fixture, and a shared superuser auth-headers fixture.

nodes.name: FakeRedis
nodes.summary: In-memory Redis substitute with TTL expiry semantics used to mock Redis during tests.

nodes.name: _pg_container
nodes.summary: Session-scoped fixture that starts a PostgresContainer via testcontainers for isolated DB tests.

nodes.name: _engine
nodes.summary: Session-scoped fixture that creates a SQLModel engine pointed at the test PostgresContainer and runs all migrations.

nodes.name: _mock_redis
nodes.summary: Autouse fixture that patches the global Redis client with FakeRedis for every test.

nodes.name: _disable_rate_limit
nodes.summary: Autouse fixture that disables the slowapi rate limiter for all tests.

nodes.name: _reset_db_engine
nodes.summary: Autouse fixture that resets the database engine state between tests.

nodes.name: session_fixture
nodes.summary: Provides a per-test SQLModel Session with transaction rollback isolation.

nodes.name: client_fixture
nodes.summary: Provides a FastAPI TestClient with the DB session dependency overridden to use the test session.

nodes.name: superuser_headers
nodes.summary: Creates a superuser via API and returns JWT auth headers for reuse across all test modules.

nodes.summary: Integration tests for UserRepository covering create, get_by_username, get_multi listing, soft delete, and exclusion of soft-deleted records.

nodes.name: _make_user
nodes.summary: Helper that creates and persists a User model instance for use in repository tests.

nodes.name: test_create_user
nodes.summary: Tests that user_repository.create stores a user and returns it with an assigned ID.

nodes.name: test_get_by_username
nodes.summary: Tests that user_repository.get_by_username retrieves the correct user.

nodes.name: test_get_multi
nodes.summary: Tests that get_multi returns multiple users with correct count.

nodes.name: test_soft_delete
nodes.summary: Tests that deleting a user sets deleted_at and returns the deleted user.

nodes.name: test_soft_delete_excluded_from_get_multi
nodes.summary: Verifies that soft-deleted users are automatically excluded from get_multi queries.

nodes.summary: Unit tests for UserService functions covering user creation, duplicate detection, superuser creation, full-name update, password update, and weak-password rejection.

nodes.name: test_create_user
nodes.summary: Tests create_user returns a persisted user with correct username.

nodes.name: test_create_duplicate_user
nodes.summary: Tests that creating a duplicate username raises ConflictException.

nodes.name: test_create_superuser
nodes.summary: Tests create_superuser sets is_superuser=True on the returned user.

nodes.name: test_update_user_full_name
nodes.summary: Tests update_user persists a full_name change correctly.

nodes.name: test_update_user_password

nodes.name: test_update_user_weak_password_raises
nodes.summary: Tests that update_user raises ValidationException when a weak password is provided.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: HTTP handlers, request/response serialization, route definitions, and FastAPI dependency injection. These files form the outermost layer that receives HTTP requests and delegates to the service layer. High fan-out (7 imports per endpoint file on average), zero fan-in — nothing imports them except the router.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Business logic, permission checks, Redis operations, module-level dependency injection, and data access via repositories. Includes service.py, repository.py, and deps.py files within each business module. These files sit between the API and data layers — they import from core/utils/models and are imported by API endpoints.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: ORM model definitions, the soft-delete base model, generic repository base class, and Alembic migration infrastructure. These files have the highest fan-in ratios (models/base.py fan-in: 4, users/models.py fan-in: 6) confirming they are foundational — many files depend on them but they depend on very little.

layers.name: Types Layer
layers.description: Pydantic/SQLModel schema definitions for request validation and response serialization. Highest fan-in file in the entire codebase is app/schemas/common.py (fan-in: 11), confirming these are widely consumed shared type contracts. Module schemas (auth, items, users) are imported by both the API layer and service layer.

layers.name: Config Layer
layers.description: Application bootstrap, infrastructure configuration, and cross-cutting concerns: settings (config.py, fan-in: 8), database sessions, Redis client, JWT security, rate limiting, structured logging, request-context storage, and operational scripts. app/main.py is the application entry point with the highest fan-out of any non-test file (8 imports).

layers.name: Middleware Layer
layers.description: Starlette middleware components that form the request/response processing pipeline (error handling, logging, request ID injection, security headers), plus the shared exceptions utility that all layers raise. app/utils/exceptions.py has a fan-in of 8 — among the highest in the codebase — confirming its cross-cutting role.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: pytest test suite covering API endpoints, repository operations, and service logic. Includes conftest.py fixtures and all test modules. These files have zero fan-in (nothing imports them) and are the only consumers of the full application stack end-to-end.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: app/main.py is the FastAPI application factory. It instantiates the FastAPI app, registers all middleware in reverse order (LIFO — last added runs first), and mounts the v1 API router. This file is the single place where the entire request pipeline is assembled, making it the best starting point for understanding how the system fits together.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI uses a LIFO middleware stack: the last middleware added via app.add_middleware() is the first to execute on each incoming request. This means the outermost concern (e.g., proxy header extraction) should be added last so it runs first, before logging and error handling.

tour.title: Configuration & Environment
tour.description: app/core/config.py defines a Pydantic BaseSettings subclass that reads all configuration from environment variables (and a .env file). It is imported by 9 other modules — the highest fan-in of any config file — making it the authoritative source for secrets, database URLs, JWT parameters, and feature flags. Copy .env.example to .env before running the app.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic BaseSettings automatically reads fields from environment variables, matching by field name (case-insensitive). This pattern gives you Python type safety and validation for all runtime configuration without any custom parsing code.

tour.title: Base Data Model & Soft Delete
tour.description: app/models/base.py defines TableBase, the SQLModel base class inherited by every ORM model in the project. TableBase adds id (UUID), created_at, updated_at, and deleted_at columns. Repository queries automatically filter out records where deleted_at is set, implementing transparent soft-delete across the entire codebase. app/modules/users/models.py shows the first concrete model built on this base.
tour.languageLesson: SQLModel merges SQLAlchemy's ORM with Pydantic's validation: a class decorated with table=True becomes both an ORM model and a Pydantic schema. TableBase uses SQLModel's inheritance mechanism to inject audit columns into every subclass without repeating code.

tour.title: Repository Pattern & Generic CRUD
tour.description: app/repositories/base.py implements RepositoryBase[ModelType], a generic class that provides type-safe create, read, update, delete, list, and paginate operations for any SQLModel table. Each module creates its own repository by subclassing RepositoryBase with its model type. This eliminates boilerplate data-access code while keeping queries testable and composable.
tour.languageLesson: Python's typing.Generic[T] allows a class to be parameterized by a type variable. RepositoryBase[ModelType] uses this so that methods like get() return the correct model type (e.g., User or Item) rather than a plain object, giving full IDE and mypy type checking throughout the codebase.

tour.title: Authentication — JWT Dual-Token & Redis Revocation
tour.description: app/modules/auth/service.py orchestrates login, token refresh, and logout. app/core/security.py handles low-level JWT creation and verification with timing-attack-resistant comparison. Access tokens expire after 30 minutes; refresh tokens last 7 days and are tracked in Redis by their jti (JWT ID). Calling logout stores the jti in Redis, making the refresh token immediately invalid even before expiry.
tour.languageLesson: The dual-token pattern separates concerns: short-lived access tokens reduce exposure if intercepted, while long-lived refresh tokens enable seamless renewal. Redis-based jti revocation solves the stateless JWT problem — you can invalidate a specific token without invalidating all tokens for a user.

tour.title: Dependency Injection with FastAPI Depends()
tour.description: app/api/deps.py exports SessionDep (database session), CurrentUser (authenticated user from JWT), and TokenDep (raw token string). app/modules/users/deps.py builds on these to provide higher-level dependencies like getting a user by path parameter. FastAPI resolves these as a composition tree before each request handler runs, handling teardown (session close) automatically.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI's Depends() implements constructor injection: each dependency can itself declare further dependencies, forming a directed acyclic graph that FastAPI resolves and caches within a single request. Generator dependencies (using yield) allow setup/teardown logic — the session is opened before the handler and closed after, even on exception.

tour.title: API Endpoints — Auth & Items
tour.description: app/api/v1/endpoints/auth.py and app/api/v1/endpoints/items.py are the thinnest layer in the four-layer architecture. Each route function declares its dependencies via Depends(), delegates all business logic to a service, and returns a serialized schema. The auth endpoints also apply rate limiting via slowapi. These files have the highest fan-out in the codebase because they wire together schemas, services, deps, and exceptions.

tour.title: Pydantic Schemas & Shared Response Types
tour.description: app/modules/users/schemas.py defines the Pydantic models for user request and response payloads. app/schemas/common.py provides shared types used across the entire API — pagination envelopes, standard error bodies, and reusable field types. app/schemas/common.py has the highest fan-in of any file in the project (11 dependents), reflecting how broadly shared response contracts are.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 validates incoming JSON against schema field types and constraints automatically. FastAPI uses the response_model parameter to filter and serialize outgoing data. Any field present in the ORM model but absent from the response schema is silently stripped, preventing accidental data leakage.

tour.title: Middleware Chain
tour.description: The middleware layer in app/middleware/ contains four focused classes: RequestIDMiddleware injects a UUID into every request as X-Request-Id; LoggingMiddleware emits structured JSON logs with that ID; SecurityHeadersMiddleware adds HTTP security headers; and ErrorHandlerMiddleware catches AppException instances and converts them to JSON responses. Each middleware shares a common type interface defined in app/middleware/_types.py.

tour.title: Exception Hierarchy
tour.description: app/utils/exceptions.py defines the AppException base class and all domain-specific subclasses (NotFoundError, UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError, ConflictError, etc.). Service and repository code raises these typed exceptions; the ErrorHandlerMiddleware catches them at the top of the stack and maps them to the appropriate HTTP status codes and JSON bodies. This keeps HTTP concerns out of business logic.

tour.title: Database Setup & Migrations
tour.description: app/core/database.py creates the SQLAlchemy async engine and session factory used by SessionDep. alembic/env.py configures Alembic for auto-generated migrations: it imports all module models so their metadata is registered, then uses SQLModel.metadata as the migration target. The initial migration at alembic/versions/20260321_0001_initial_schema.py creates the user and item tables with all soft-delete columns and indexes.

tour.title: Testing Strategy
tour.description: tests/conftest.py provides pytest fixtures for an async test client, a test database session, and pre-created user objects. tests/api/v1/test_auth.py demonstrates the API-layer testing pattern: each test sends real HTTP requests through the full middleware and route stack against an in-memory test database. The project also has service-layer tests (tests/services/) and repository-layer tests (tests/repositories/) for isolated unit coverage.
